```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Turn interior 0‑cells into 9.  A 0 becomes 9 exactly when it lies
%  strictly between the leftmost and the rightmost 2 in the same row.
%  All other cells keep their original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- cells that appear in the input -----
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ----- leftmost 2 in a row (needs a 2 on the left) -----
% use a fresh guard variable (C2) and a fresh min‑variable (C3)
leftmost(R,L) :- input(R,C2,2), L = #min { C3 : input(R,C3,2) }.

% ----- rightmost 2 in a row (needs a 2 on the right) -----
% guard with a fresh variable (C4) and a fresh max‑variable (C5)
rightmost(R,Rc) :- input(R,C4,2), Rc = #max { C5 : input(R,C5,2) }.

% ----- does the row contain any 2 ?  (deterministic count) -----
count2(R) = #count { C : input(R,C,2) }.
has2(R)      :- count2(R) > 0.

% ----- interior 0‑cells : 0 that are between the two 2‑cells -----
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    has2(R),
    leftmost(R,L),
    rightmost(R,Rc),
    L < C,
    C < Rc.

% ----- output colours ------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,9)  :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).
output(R,C,2)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% ----- cells that stay unchanged ---------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    not interior(R,C),
    color(V).

% ----- exactly one colour for each output cell -------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```
refinements.program:
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Turn interior 0‑cells into 9.  A 0 becomes 9 exactly when it lies
%  strictly between the leftmost and the rightmost 2 in the same row.
%  All other cells keep their original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- cells that appear in the input -----
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ----- leftmost 2 in a row (needs a 2 on the left) -----
leftmost(R,L) :- input(R,C2,2),
                L = #min { C3 : input(R,C3,2) }.

% ----- rightmost 2 in a row (needs a 2 on the right) -----
rightmost(R,Rc) :- input(R,C4,2),
                Rc = #max { C5 : input(R,C5,2) }.

% ----- count occurrences of 2 in a row and test presence -----
count2(R,N) :- input(R,_,_),
                N = #count { C : input(R,C,2) }.

has2(R) :- count2(R,N), N > 0.

% ----- interior 0‑cells : 0 that are between the two 2‑cells -----
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    has2(R),
    leftmost(R,L),
    rightmost(R,Rc),
    L < C,
    C < Rc.

% ----- output colours (turn interior 0‑cells into 9) -----
output(R,C,9) :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

output(R,C,2) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% ----- cells that stay unchanged ---------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    not interior(R,C),
    color(V).

% ----- exactly one colour for each output cell -------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Transformation rule inferred from the three training examples:
%   For each row, the leftmost and rightmost occurrence of colour 2
%   define a horizontal segment. Every cell strictly between those
%   two 2‑cells that currently has colour 0 is turned into colour 9.
%   All other cells keep their original colour.  The interior cells
%   that already are colour 2 stay as 2.  This produces the exact
%   output shown in the three examples.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ---------- Grid dimensions ----------
maxRow(M)      :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
height(H)      :- H = 1 + M, maxRow(M).

maxCol(N)      :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
width(W)       :- W = 1 + N, maxCol(N).

% ---------- Output domain ----------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ---------- Rows that contain a 2 ----------
row_has_2(R)   :- input(R,C,2).

% ---------- Leftmost / rightmost 2 in a row ----------
leftmost(R,L)  :- row_has_2(R), L = #min { C : input(R,C,2) }.
rightmost(R,Rc):- row_has_2(R), Rc = #max { C : input(R,C,2) }.

% ---------- Cells strictly between the two 2s ----------
interior(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), leftmost(R,L), rightmost(R,Rc), L < C, C < Rc.

% ---------- Output rules ----------
% 1) Cells outside the interior keep their original colour.
output(R,C,V)  :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), not interior(R,C), color(V).

% 2) Interior zeros become 9.
output(R,C,9)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), output_cell(R,C), color(0).

% 3) Interior 2s stay 2 (otherwise they would be omitted).
output(R,C,2)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% ---------- Exactly one output per output cell ----------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Grid puzzle – turn interior 0‑cells into 9.
%  For every row we look at the leftmost and the rightmost 2.
%  Every 0 that lies strictly between those two 2’s becomes 9.
%  All other cells keep their original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ---------- Grid dimensions ----------
maxRow(M)       :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
height(H)       :- H = 1 + M, maxRow(M).

maxCol(N)       :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
width(W)        :- W = 1 + N, maxCol(N).

% ---------- Output domain ----------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ---------- Row contains a 2 ----------
row_has_2(R)    :- input(R,C,2).

% ---------- Leftmost / rightmost 2 in a row ----------
leftmost(R,L)   :- row_has_2(R), L = #min { C : input(R,C,2) }.
rightmost(R,Rc) :- row_has_2(R), Rc = #max { C : input(R,C,2) }.

% ---------- Cells that must become 9 ----------
interior(R,C)   :- output_cell(R,C),
                  input(R,C,0),
                  leftmost(R,L),
                  rightmost(R,Rc),
                  L < C,
                  C < Rc.

% ---------- Helper predicates for safety ----------
row(R)        :- output_cell(R,C).
col(C)        :- output_cell(R,C).

% ---------- Output rules ----------
% 1) Cells outside the interior keep their original colour.
output(R,C,V)   :- output_cell(R,C),
                  row(R), col(C),
                  not interior(R,C),
                  color(V).

% 2) Interior zeros become 9.
output(R,C,9)   :- interior(R,C),
                  input(R,C,0),
                  output_cell(R,C),
                  color(0).

% 3) Interior 2s stay 2 (otherwise they would be omitted).
output(R,C,2)   :- interior(R,C),
                  input(R,C,2),
                  output_cell(R,C),
                  color(2).

% ---------- Exactly one colour per output cell ----------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
% Compute max dimensions safely (binding variables positively)
maxHeight(H) :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }, H = M + 1.

maxWidth(W) :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }, W = N + 1.

% any cell that appears in the input
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% helper predicates for left and right 2‑cells
%   add a positive literal so that R and C are safe under negation
left_ok(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), L = #count { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 < C }, L > 1.

right_ok(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), rc = #count { C2 : input(R,C2,2), C2 > C }, rc > 1.

% a zero becomes 9 exactly when there are at least two 2‑cells on the left
% and at least two 2‑cells on the right of the cell in the same row.
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),                     % cell present
    input(R,C,0),                         % currently 0
    left_ok(R,C),
    right_ok(R,C).

% output rules
output(R,C,9)  :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(0).

output(R,C,2)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

output(R,C,V)  :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), not interior(R,C), color(V).

% exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Puzzle: turn 0‑cells into 9 exactly when they are
% between a 2‑cell and the next 2‑cell on the right,
% with the distances to the nearest 2 on each side being
% 1 or 2 columns.
% All other cells keep their original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- Grid cells -----
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ----- Nearest 2 to the left of a cell (the greatest column < C with a 2) -----
nearest_left(R,C,L) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    L = #max { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 < C }.

% ----- Nearest 2 to the right of a cell (the smallest column > C with a 2) -----
nearest_right(R,C,Rc) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    Rc = #min { C2 : input(R,C2,2), C2 > C }.

% ----- Check distances (must be 1 or 2 on each side) -----
check_dist(R,C,L,Rc) :-
    true,
    (C = L + 1 ; C = L + 2),
    (Rc = C + 1 ; Rc = C + 2).

% ----- Zeros that must become 9.
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    nearest_left(R,C,L),
    nearest_right(R,C,Rc),
    check_dist(R,C,L,Rc).

% ----- Output colours -----
output(R,C,9) :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).
output(R,C,2) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).
output(R,C,V) :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), not interior(R,C), color(V).

% ----- Exactly one colour per output cell -----
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Grid puzzle – turn a 0 into 9 exactly when a 2 is
%  at distance 1 or 2 on both its left and right side.
%  All other cells keep their original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- Compute max row index and height -----
max_row(M)      :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
height(H)       :- max_row(M), H = M + 1.
hasHeight(H)    :- height(H).                % makes H safely bound

% ----- Compute max column index and width -----
max_col(N)      :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
width(W)        :- max_col(N), W = N + 1.
hasWidth(W)     :- width(W).                  % makes W safely bound

% ----- Cells that appear in the input -----
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ----- Count of 2‑cells within distance 2 on the left -----
leftCount(R,C,Lc) :- output_cell(R,C),
                     Lc = #count {
                       C1 : input(R,C1,2),
                       C1 < C,
                       C - C1 <= 2
                     }.

% ----- Count of 2‑cells within distance 2 on the right -----
rightCount(R,C,Rc) :- output_cell(R,C),
                      Rc = #count {
                       C1 : input(R,C1,2),
                       C1 > C,
                       C1 - C <= 2
                     }.

% ----- Interior cells (zeros that have a 2 close on both sides) -----
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    leftCount(R,C,Lc),
    rightCount(R,C,Rc),
    Lc > 0,
    Rc > 0.

% ----- Output colours for interior cells -----
output(R,C,9)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), output_cell(R,C), color(9).
output(R,C,2)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% ----- Non‑interior cells keep their original colour -----
output(R,C,V)  :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                  not interior(R,C), color(V).

% ----- Exactly one colour per output cell -----
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Turn interior 0‑cells into 9.  A 0 becomes 9 exactly when it lies
%  strictly between the leftmost and the rightmost 2 in the same row.
%  All other cells keep their original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- cells that appear in the input -----
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ----- leftmost 2 in a row (needs a 2 on the left) -----
leftmost(R,L) :- input(R,C2,2),
                L = #min { C3 : input(R,C3,2) }.

% ----- rightmost 2 in a row (needs a 2 on the right) -----
rightmost(R,Rc) :- input(R,C4,2),
                Rc = #max { C5 : input(R,C5,2) }.

% ----- count occurrences of 2 in a row and test presence -----
count2(R,N) :- input(R,_,_),
                N = #count { C : input(R,C,2) }.

has2(R) :- count2(R,N), N > 0.

% ----- interior 0‑cells : 0 that are between the two 2‑cells -----
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    has2(R),
    leftmost(R,L),
    rightmost(R,Rc),
    L < C,
    C < Rc.

% ----- output colours (turn interior 0‑cells into 9) -----
output(R,C,9) :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

output(R,C,2) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% ----- cells that stay unchanged ---------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    not interior(R,C),
    color(V).

% ----- exactly one colour for each output cell -------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

Attempt5 code:

```asp
% Compute max row index and height
max_row(M) :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
height(H) :- max_row(M), H = M + 1.
hasHeight(H) :- height(H).

% Compute max column index and width
max_col(N) :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
width(W) :- max_col(N), W = N + 1.
hasWidth(W) :- width(W).

% any cell that appears in the input
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% helper predicates for left and right 2-cells within distance 2
leftCount(R,C,Lc) :- output_cell(R,C),
                     Lc = #count {
                       C1 : input(R,C1,2),
                       C1 < C,
                       C - C1 <= 2
                     }.

rightCount(R,C,Rc) :- output_cell(R,C),
                      Rc = #count {
                       C1 : input(R,C1,2),
                       C1 > C,
                       C1 - C <= 2
                     }.

% Interior cells (zeros that have a 2 close on both sides)
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    leftCount(R,C,Lc),
    rightCount(R,C,Rc),
    Lc > 0,
    Rc > 0.

% output colours for interior cells
output(R,C,9)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), output_cell(R,C), color(9).
output(R,C,2)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% Non‑interior cells keep original colour
output(R,C,V) :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                  not interior(R,C), color(V).

% exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

Let's craft final code:

```asp
% Grid dimensions (optional)
maxRow(M) :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
height(H)    :- maxRow(M), H = M + 1.
maxCol(N) :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
width(W)    :- maxCol(N), W = N + 1.

% cells that appear in the input
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% count of 2's to the left of (R,C) within distance 2
leftCount(R,C,Lc) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    Lc = #count { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 < C, C - C1 <= 2 }.

% count of 2's to the right of (R,C) within distance 2
rightCount(R,C,Rc) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    Rc = #count { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 > C, C1 - C <= 2 }.

% interior zeros: zeros that have a 2 nearby on both sides
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    leftCount(R,C,Lc),
    rightCount(R,C,Rc),
    Lc > 0,
    Rc > 0.

% turn interior zeros into 9
output(R,C,9) :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

% turn interior 2's into 9 (not actually needed, but keeps original)
output(R,C,2) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% keep other cells unchanged (they already have their original colour)
output(R,C,V) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    not interior(R,C),
    color(V).

% exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
% Grid puzzle – turn a 0 into 9 exactly when it has a 2
% within distance 2 on the left and a 2 within distance 2 on the right.
% All other cells keep their original colour.

% ---- cells appearing in the input ----
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ---- count 2's to the left of (R,C) within distance 2 ----
leftCount(R,C,Lc) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    Lc = #count { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 < C, C - C1 <= 2 }.

% ---- count 2's to the right of (R,C) within distance 2 ----
rightCount(R,C,Rc) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    Rc = #count { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 > C, C1 - C <= 2 }.

% ---- interior zeros: have a nearby 2 on both sides ----
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    leftCount(R,C,Lc),
    rightCount(R,C,Rc),
    Lc > 0,
    Rc > 0.

% ---- turn interior zeros into 9 ----
output(R,C,9) :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

% ---- interior 2's keep 2 (not actually needed, but harmless) ----
output(R,C,2) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% ---- keep other cells unchanged (use their original colour) ----
output(R,C,V) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    not interior(R,C),
    color(V).

% ---- exactly one colour for each output cell ----
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
% Grid puzzle – turn a 0 into 9 exactly when it has a 2 within distance 2 on both sides.
% All other cells keep their original colour.

% ------- cells appearing in the input -------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ------- count 2's to the left of (R,C) within distance 2 -------
leftCount(R,C,Lc) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    Lc = #count { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 < C, C - C1 <= 2 }.

% ------- count 2's to the right of (R,C) within distance 2 -------
rightCount(R,C,Rc) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    Rc = #count { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 > C, C1 - C <= 2 }.

% ------- interior zeros: have a nearby 2 on both sides -------
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    leftCount(R,C,Lc),
    rightCount(R,C,Rc),
    Lc > 0,
    Rc > 0.

% ------- turn interior zeros into 9 -------
output(R,C,9) :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

% ------- interior 2's stay 2 (harmless) -------
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Transformation rule inferred from the three training examples:
%   For each row, the leftmost and rightmost occurrence of colour 2
%   define a horizontal segment. Every cell strictly between those
%   two 2‑cells that currently has colour 0 is turned into colour 9.
%   All other cells keep their original colour.  The interior cells
%   that already are colour 2 stay as 2.  This produces the exact
%   output shown in the three examples.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ---------- Grid dimensions ----------
maxRow(M)      :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
height(H)      :- H = 1 + M, maxRow(M).

maxCol(N)      :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
width(W)       :- W = 1 + N, maxCol(N).

% ---------- Output domain ----------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ---------- Rows that contain a 2 ----------
row_has_2(R)   :- input(R,C,2).

% ---------- Leftmost / rightmost 2 in a row ----------
leftmost(R,L)  :- row_has_2(R), L = #min { C : input(R,C,2) }.
rightmost(R,Rc):- row_has_2(R), Rc = #max { C : input(R,C,2) }.

% ---------- Cells strictly between the two 2s ----------
interior(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), leftmost(R,L), rightmost(R,Rc), L < C, C < Rc.

% ---------- Output rules ----------
% 1) Cells outside the interior keep their original colour.
output(R,C,V)  :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), not interior(R,C), color(V).

% 2) Interior zeros become 9.
output(R,C,9)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), output_cell(R,C), color(0).

% 3) Interior 2s stay 2 (otherwise they would be omitted).
output(R,C,2)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% ---------- Exactly one output per output cell ----------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Puzzle: turn 0‑cells into 9 exactly when they are
% between a 2‑cell and the next 2‑cell on the right,
% with the distances to the nearest 2 on each side being
% 1 or 2 columns.
% All other cells keep their original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- Grid cells -----
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ----- Nearest 2 to the left of a cell (the greatest column < C with a 2) -----
nearest_left(R,C,L) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    L = #max { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 < C }.

% ----- Nearest 2 to the right of a cell (the smallest column > C with a 2) -----
nearest_right(R,C,Rc) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    Rc = #min { C2 : input(R,C2,2), C2 > C }.

% ----- Check distances (must be 1 or 2 on each side) -----
check_dist(R,C,L,Rc) :-
    true,
    (C = L + 1 ; C = L + 2),
    (Rc = C + 1 ; Rc = C + 2).

% ----- Zeros that must become 9.
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    nearest_left(R,C,L),
    nearest_right(R,C,Rc),
    check_dist(R,C,L,Rc).

% ----- Output colours -----
output(R,C,9) :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).
output(R,C,2) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).
output(R,C,V) :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), not interior(R,C), color(V).

% ----- Exactly one colour per output cell -----
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
leftmost(R,L) :- input(R,C2,2),
                L = #min { C3 : input(R,C3,2) }.

% ----- rightmost 2 in a row (needs a 2 on the right) -----
rightmost(R,Rc) :- input(R,C4,2),
                Rc = #max { C5 : input(R,C5,2) }.

% ----- count occurrences of 2 in a row and test presence -----
count2(R,N) :- input(R,_,_),
                N = #count { C : input(R,C,2) }.

has2(R) :- count2(R,N), N > 0.

% ----- interior 0‑cells : 0 that are between the two 2‑cells -----
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    has2(R),
    leftmost(R,L),
    rightmost(R,Rc),
    L < C,
    C < Rc.

% ----- output colours (turn interior 0‑cells into 9) -----
output(R,C,9) :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

output(R,C,2) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% ----- cells that stay unchanged ---------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    not interior(R,C),
    color(V).

% ----- exactly one colour for each output cell -------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
% Grid puzzle – turn a 0 into 9 exactly when it has a 2 within distance 2 on both sides.
% All other cells keep their original colour.

% ----- cells that appear in the input -----
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ----- count of 2‑cells to the left of (R,C) within distance 2 -----
leftCount(R,C,Lc) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    Lc = #count {
        C1 : input(R,C1,2),
        C1 < C,
        C - C1 <= 2
    }.

% ----- count of 2‑cells to the right of (R,C) within distance 2 -----
rightCount(R,C,Rc) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    Rc = #count {
        C1 : input(R,C1,2),
        C1 > C,
        C1 - C <= 2
    }.

% ----- interior zeros: have a 2 close on the left and on the right -----
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    leftCount(R,C,Lc),
    rightCount(R,C,Rc),
    Lc > 0,
    Rc > 0.

% ----- turn interior zeros into 9 -----
output(R,C,9) :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

% ----- interior 2’s stay 2 (harmless, keeps the 2) -----
output(R,C,2) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% ----- keep other cells unchanged (they already have their original colour) -----
output(R,C,V) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    not interior(R,C),
    color(V).

% ----- exactly one colour for each output cell -----
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

% Dimensions
maxRow(M) :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
height(H) :- maxRow(M), H = M + 1.
maxCol(N) :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
width(W) :- maxCol(N), W = N + 1.

% output cells
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% find leftmost and rightmost 2 in a row
leftmost(R,L) :- input(R,C2,2), L = #min { C3 : input(R,C3,2) }.
rightmost(R,Rc) :- input(R,C4,2), Rc = #max { C5 : input(R,C5,2) }.

% interior zeros
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    leftmost(R,L),
    rightmost(R,Rc),
    L < C,
    C < Rc,
    Z = #count {
        C0 : output_cell(R,C0),
        input(R,C0,0),
        C0 > L,
        C0 < Rc,
        C0 != C
    },
    Z > 0.

% output rules
output(R,C,9)  :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).
output(R,C,2)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).
output(R,C,V)  :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                  not interior(R,C), color(V).

% exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

refinements.response:
```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Grid puzzle – turn a 0 into 9 exactly when it lies strictly
% between two 2‑cells in the same row and there is at least one
% other 0 between those two 2‑cells.  All other cells keep their
% original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- Grid dimensions -----
maxRow(M)    :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
height(H)    :- maxRow(M), H = M + 1.

maxCol(N)    :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
width(W)     :- maxCol(N), W = N + 1.

% ----- Cells that appear in the input -----
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ----- Leftmost and rightmost 2 in a row -----
leftmost(R,L)  :- input(R,C2,2),
                L = #min { C3 : input(R,C3,2) }.

rightmost(R,Rc):- input(R,C4,2),
                Rc = #max { C5 : input(R,C5,2) }.

% ----- Interior 0‑cells (turn to 9) -----
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    leftmost(R,L),
    rightmost(R,Rc),
    L < C,
    C < Rc,
    Z = #count {
        C0 : output_cell(R,C0),
        input(R,C0,0),
        C0 > L,
        C0 < Rc,
        C0 != C
    },
    Z > 0.

% ----- Output colours -----
output(R,C,9)  :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).
output(R,C,2)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).
output(R,C,V)  :- output_cell(R,C),
                  input(R,C,V),
                  not interior(R,C),
                  color(V).

% ----- Exactly one colour per output cell -----
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

% Grid puzzle – turn a 0 into 9 exactly when it lies strictly
% between two 2‑cells in the same row and there is at least one
% other 0 between those two 2‑cells.  All other cells keep their
% original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- Grid dimensions -----

height(H)    :- maxRow(M), H = M + 1.

maxCol(N)    :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
width(W)     :- maxCol(N), W = N + 1.

% ----- Cells that appear in the input -----

output(R,C,V)  :- output_cell(R,C),
                  input(R,C,V),

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Transformation rule inferred from the three training examples:
%   For each row, the leftmost and rightmost occurrence of colour 2
%   define a horizontal segment. Every cell strictly between those
%   two 2‑cells that currently has colour 0 is turned into colour 9.
%   All other cells keep their original colour.  The interior cells
%   that already are colour 2 stay as 2.  This produces the exact
%   output shown in the three examples.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ---------- Grid dimensions ----------
maxRow(M)      :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
height(H)      :- H = 1 + M, maxRow(M).

maxCol(N)      :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
width(W)       :- W = 1 + N, maxCol(N).

% ---------- Output domain ----------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ---------- Rows that contain a 2 ----------
row_has_2(R)   :- input(R,C,2).

% ---------- Leftmost / rightmost 2 in a row ----------
leftmost(R,L)  :- row_has_2(R), L = #min { C : input(R,C,2) }.
rightmost(R,Rc):- row_has_2(R), Rc = #max { C : input(R,C,2) }.

% ---------- Cells strictly between the two 2s ----------
interior(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), leftmost(R,L), rightmost(R,Rc), L < C, C < Rc.

% 1) Cells outside the interior keep their original colour.

% 2) Interior zeros become 9.

% 3) Interior 2s stay 2 (otherwise they would be omitted).
output(R,C,2)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% ---------- Exactly one output per output cell ----------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Puzzle: turn 0‑cells into 9 exactly when they are
% between a 2‑cell and the next 2‑cell on the right,
% with the distances to the nearest 2 on each side being
% 1 or 2 columns.
% All other cells keep their original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- Grid cells -----
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ----- Nearest 2 to the left of a cell (the greatest column < C with a 2) -----
nearest_left(R,C,L) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    L = #max { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 < C }.

% ----- Nearest 2 to the right of a cell (the smallest column > C with a 2) -----
nearest_right(R,C,Rc) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    Rc = #min { C2 : input(R,C2,2), C2 > C }.

% ----- Check distances (must be 1 or 2 on each side) -----
check_dist(R,C,L,Rc) :-
    true,
    (C = L + 1 ; C = L + 2),
    (Rc = C + 1 ; Rc = C + 2).

% ----- Zeros that must become 9.
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    nearest_left(R,C,L),
    nearest_right(R,C,Rc),
    check_dist(R,C,L,Rc).

% ----- Output colours -----
output(R,C,9) :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).
output(R,C,2) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).
output(R,C,V) :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), not interior(R,C), color(V).

% ----- Exactly one colour per output cell -----
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
%  at distance 1 or 2 on both its left and right side.
%  All other cells keep their original colour.

% ----- Compute max row index and height -----
max_row(M)      :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.

% ----- Compute max column index and width -----
max_col(N)      :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

                     Lc = #count {

                       C - C1 <= 2
                     }.

% ----- Count of 2‑cells within distance 2 on the right -----
rightCount(R,C,Rc) :- output_cell(R,C),

                       C1 > C,
                       C1 - C <= 2

output(R,C,2)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% ----- Non‑interior cells keep their original colour -----
output(R,C,V)  :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                  not interior(R,C), color(V).

% ----- Exactly one colour per output cell -----
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Turn interior 0‑cells into 9.  A 0 becomes 9 exactly when it lies
%  strictly between the leftmost and the rightmost 2 in the same row.
%  All other cells keep their original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- cells that appear in the input -----
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ----- leftmost 2 in a row (needs a 2 on the left) -----
leftmost(R,L) :- input(R,C2,2),
                L = #min { C3 : input(R,C3,2) }.

% ----- rightmost 2 in a row (needs a 2 on the right) -----
rightmost(R,Rc) :- input(R,C4,2),
                Rc = #max { C5 : input(R,C5,2) }.

% ----- count occurrences of 2 in a row and test presence -----
count2(R,N) :- input(R,_,_),
                N = #count { C : input(R,C,2) }.

has2(R) :- count2(R,N), N > 0.

% ----- interior 0‑cells : 0 that are between the two 2‑cells -----
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    has2(R),
    leftmost(R,L),
    rightmost(R,Rc),
    L < C,
    C < Rc.

% ----- output colours (turn interior 0‑cells into 9) -----
output(R,C,9) :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

output(R,C,2) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% ----- cells that stay unchanged ---------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    not interior(R,C),
    color(V).

% ----- exactly one colour for each output cell -------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
interior(R,C) :-

    leftCount(R,C,Lc),
    rightCount(R,C,Rc),

    Rc > 0.

% ----- turn interior zeros into 9 -----
output(R,C,9) :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

% ----- interior 2’s stay 2 (harmless, keeps the 2) -----
output(R,C,2) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% ----- keep other cells unchanged (they already have their original colour) -----
output(R,C,V) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    not interior(R,C),
    color(V).

% ----- exactly one colour for each output cell -----
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Grid puzzle – turn a 0 into 9 exactly when it lies strictly
% between two 2‑cells in the same row and there is at least one
% other 0 between those two 2‑cells.  All other cells keep their
% original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- Grid dimensions -----
maxRow(M)    :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
height(H)    :- maxRow(M), H = M + 1.

maxCol(N)    :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
width(W)     :- maxCol(N), W = N + 1.

% ----- Cells that appear in the input -----
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ----- Leftmost and rightmost 2 in a row -----
leftmost(R,L)  :- input(R,C2,2),
                L = #min { C3 : input(R,C3,2) }.

rightmost(R,Rc):- input(R,C4,2),
                Rc = #max { C5 : input(R,C5,2) }.

% ----- Interior 0‑cells (turn to 9) -----
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    leftmost(R,L),
    rightmost(R,Rc),
    L < C,
    C < Rc,
    Z = #count {
        C0 : output_cell(R,C0),
        input(R,C0,0),
        C0 > L,
        C0 < Rc,
        C0 != C
    },
    Z > 0.

% ----- Output colours -----
output(R,C,9)  :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).
output(R,C,2)  :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).
output(R,C,V)  :- output_cell(R,C),
                  input(R,C,V),
                  not interior(R,C),
                  color(V).

% ----- Exactly one colour per output cell -----
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

% Dimensions (optional)
maxRow(M) :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(N) :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    L = #max { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 < C },
    L > 0,
    Rc = #min { C2 : input(R,C2,2), C2 > C },
    Rc > 0,
    L < C,
    C < Rc.

output(R,C,9) :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

output(R,C,2) :- input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

output(R,C,V) :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), not interior(R,C), color(V).

% exactly one colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Turn a 0 into 9 exactly when it lies between two 2‑cells
% in the same row (the nearest 2 on the left and the nearest 2 on the right).
% All other cells keep their original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% grid dimensions (optional)
maxRow(M)   :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(N)   :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    L = #max { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 < C },
    L > 0,
    Rc = #min { C2 : input(R,C2,2), C2 > C },
    Rc > 0,
    L < C,
    C < Rc.

output(R,C,9)  :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

output(R,C,2)  :- input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

output(R,C,V)  :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), not interior(R,C), color(V).

% Exactly one colour for every cell that appears in the input
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
% Grid puzzle – turn a 0 into 9 exactly when it has a 2
% on the left and a 2 on the right in the same row (the nearest
% 2 on each side). All other cells keep their original colour.

% ---------- Grid dimensions (optional) ----------
maxRow(M)  :- M = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxCol(N)  :- N = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    L = #max { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 < C },
    L > 0,
    Rc = #min { C2 : input(R,C2,2), C2 > C },
    Rc > 0,
    L < C,
    C < Rc.

output(R,C,9)  :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

output(R,C,2)  :- input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

output(R,C,V)  :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), not interior(R,C), color(V).

% Exactly one colour for each cell that occurs in the input
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Turn a 0 into 9 exactly when it has a 2 to the left
% and a 2 to the right in the same row (nearest on each side).
% All other cells keep their original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    L = #max { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 < C },
    L > 0,
    Rc = #min { C2 : input(R,C2,2), C2 > C },
    Rc > 0,
    L < C,
    C < Rc.

output(R,C,9)  :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

output(R,C,2)  :- input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

output(R,C,V) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    not interior(R,C),
    V != 2,
    color(V).

% Exactly one colour for each cell that appears in the input
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

refinements.response:
```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Turn a 0 into 9 exactly when it has a 2 to the left
% and a 2 to the right in the same row (the nearest 2 on each side).
% All other cells keep their original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% cells that appear in the input
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% a zero is interior iff there is a 2 immediately to its left
% and a 2 immediately to its right in the same row
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    L = #max { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 < C },
    L > 0,
    Rc = #min { C2 : input(R,C2,2), C2 > C },
    Rc > 0,
    L < C,
    C < Rc.

% turn interior zeros into 9
output(R,C,9)  :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

% keep original 2‑cells as 2
output(R,C,2)  :- input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% keep all other cells unchanged
output(R,C,V) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    not interior(R,C),
    V != 2,                % avoid duplicating colour 2
    color(V).

% exactly one colour for every cell that appears in the input
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
```

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Turn a 0 into 9 exactly when it has a 2 to the left
% and a 2 to the right in the same row (the nearest 2 on each side).
% All other cells keep their original colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% cells that appear in the input
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% a zero is interior iff there is a 2 immediately to its left
% and a 2 immediately to its right in the same row
interior(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,0),
    L = #max { C1 : input(R,C1,2), C1 < C },
    L > 0,
    Rc = #min { C2 : input(R,C2,2), C2 > C },
    Rc > 0,
    L < C,
    C < Rc.

% turn interior zeros into 9
output(R,C,9)  :- interior(R,C), output_cell(R,C), color(9).

% keep original 2‑cells as 2
output(R,C,2)  :- input(R,C,2), output_cell(R,C), color(2).

% keep all other cells unchanged
output(R,C,V) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    not interior(R,C),
    V != 2,                % avoid duplicating colour 2
    color(V).

% exactly one colour for every cell that appears in the input
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
